description: Constitutional alignment proofs: prove similarity_score ≥ threshold without revealing the score. Pedersen commitments + Schnorr OR proofs over Ristretto255, 14-bit range proof via bit decomposition + homomorphic sum check.

protocol.pedersen_h_hash_to_curve_note: Rust uses SHA3-512 hash-to-curve; this build uses RFC 9380 (SHA-512). H bytes differ between implementations, protocol shape identical.
fields_revealed_to_verifier: per-bit Schnorr OR proofs (a0, a1, e0, s0, e1, s1)
fields_hidden_from_verifier: which Schnorr OR branch was the real one

records.title: High alignment — clean pass
records.plain: A safe read action with strong alignment. Score 0.92, threshold 0.75. Verifier learns the score is at-or-above 0.75. Verifier does not learn it was 0.92 specifically.

beats.copy: Score on the cosine-similarity side: 0.92. The prover knows this exactly.
Fixed-point encode: score × 10000 = 9200. Threshold × 10000 = 7500. Fits in 14 bits.
Decompose into 14 bits. For each bit b_i, commit C_i = b_i·G + r_i·H — 14 fresh Pedersen commits, one per bit.
Per bit, prove "b_i is 0 or 1" with a Schnorr OR proof. One branch is the real proof; the other is simulated with a forced challenge. The verifier cannot tell which.
Value commitment = Σ(2^i · C_i). The homomorphism ties the bits together — verifier can re-derive this sum from the bundle.
Verifier receives the bundle. Re-hashes Fiat-Shamir per bit, checks both branch equations, checks the homomorphic sum.

All 14 bit-proofs pass. Sum check passes. Cryptographic verdict: prover knew a valid 14-bit score (committed to C_value). Threshold-binding (score ≥ 0.75, well above) is enforced by the prover-side refusal-to-create rule — see "honest framing" below.

records.title: Barely above — tight pass
records.plain: Score 0.76 with threshold 0.75. The proof verifies, and the verifier still cannot distinguish 0.76 from 0.92 — both look identical from the bundle.

beats.copy: Score on the cosine-similarity side: 0.76. The prover knows this exactly.
Fixed-point encode: score × 10000 = 7600. Threshold × 10000 = 7500. Fits in 14 bits.
Decompose into 14 bits. For each bit b_i, commit C_i = b_i·G + r_i·H — 14 fresh Pedersen commits, one per bit.
Per bit, prove "b_i is 0 or 1" with a Schnorr OR proof. One branch is the real proof; the other is simulated with a forced challenge. The verifier cannot tell which.
Value commitment = Σ(2^i · C_i). The homomorphism ties the bits together — verifier can re-derive this sum from the bundle.
Verifier receives the bundle. Re-hashes Fiat-Shamir per bit, checks both branch equations, checks the homomorphic sum.

All 14 bit-proofs pass. Sum check passes. Cryptographic verdict: prover knew a valid 14-bit score (committed to C_value). Threshold-binding (score ≥ 0.75, one fixed-point unit above) is enforced by the prover-side refusal-to-create rule — see "honest framing" below.

records.title: Exactly at threshold — boundary
records.plain: Score equals threshold to four decimal places. The proof permits ≥, so this verifies; the boundary case demonstrates the inequality is non-strict in fixed-point.

beats.copy: Score on the cosine-similarity side: 0.75. The prover knows this exactly.
Fixed-point encode: score × 10000 = 7500. Threshold × 10000 = 7500. Fits in 14 bits.
Decompose into 14 bits. For each bit b_i, commit C_i = b_i·G + r_i·H — 14 fresh Pedersen commits, one per bit.
Per bit, prove "b_i is 0 or 1" with a Schnorr OR proof. One branch is the real proof; the other is simulated with a forced challenge. The verifier cannot tell which.
Value commitment = Σ(2^i · C_i). The homomorphism ties the bits together — verifier can re-derive this sum from the bundle.
Verifier receives the bundle. Re-hashes Fiat-Shamir per bit, checks both branch equations, checks the homomorphic sum.

All 14 bit-proofs pass. Sum check passes. Cryptographic verdict: prover knew a valid 14-bit score (committed to C_value). Threshold-binding (score ≥ 0.75, exactly at) is enforced by the prover-side refusal-to-create rule — see "honest framing" below.

records.title: Below threshold — creation refused
records.plain: Score 0.62 < threshold 0.75. The prover cannot generate a valid alignment proof at all — the protocol refuses to construct one. No bundle ever reaches the verifier.

beats.copy: Score on the cosine-similarity side: 0.62. The prover knows this exactly.
Fixed-point encode: score × 10000 = 6200. Threshold × 10000 = 7500. Fits in 14 bits.
Pre-check: is 6200 ≥ 7500? No. AlignmentProof::create returns ZkError::ValueOutOfRange.
No bundle is constructed and nothing is sent. The verifier sees nothing — that is the strongest possible refusal.
Notice the negative space: a misaligned action never even reaches the wire. The protocol cannot lie about its own score.

records.title: Tampered proof — verifier rejects
records.plain: A valid proof is created at score 0.88. An attacker swaps one bit commitment with a fresh Pedersen commit. The per-bit Schnorr OR proof no longer matches its commitment — verifier rejects with a precise reason.

beats.copy: Score on the cosine-similarity side: 0.88. The prover knows this exactly.
Fixed-point encode: score × 10000 = 8800. Threshold × 10000 = 7500. Fits in 14 bits.
Per bit: commit C_i = b_i·G + r_i·H, then prove b_i ∈ {0,1} with a Schnorr OR — one real branch, one simulated, indistinguishable on the wire.
Fiat-Shamir collapses the interactive challenge into one SHA3-512 hash over (C_i, a_0, a_1). All 14 bit proofs ship.
Attacker intercepts and swaps bit-3's commitment with a fresh Pedersen point. Same shape, different secret.
Verifier re-derives Fiat-Shamir on bit 3: SHA3-512(C', a_0, a_1) gives a new challenge e'. But the stored e_0 + e_1 was bound to the old C. e_0 + e_1 ≠ e'. Bundle rejected.
